commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens trusted-tool-policy registry reads, policy evaluation, decision normalization, and before-tool-call diagnostics, with focused regression coverage for malformed policy cases.

PR surface: Source +352, Tests +358. Total +710 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/test level: current main directly reads and evaluates trusted policy state without the new guarded normalization, and the PR adds focused failing-shape tests for those paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is required because the PR has a protected maintainer label and intentionally changes compatibility-sensitive trusted-policy fail-closed behavior; no narrow automation repair is indicated.

target.title: fix(plugins): fail closed on trusted policy errors
target.body:
## Summary

- fails closed when trusted tool policy registration, registry iteration, policy evaluation, decision fields, or policy params are unreadable or malformed
- preserves compatible no-op policy results such as `undefined`, optional fields, and empty `{}` decisions
- keeps before-tool-call diagnostics on guarded policy snapshots instead of raw registry reads
- intentionally prunes deeper approval-option enum policing so this stays focused on crash/fail-open prevention
- uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures in tests and PR notes

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 2 routed shards, 113 tests passed on final rebased head `b19b22bf5a`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- touched-file scrub for reported plugin/tool names, private paths, and private network literals: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean after final rebase, no accepted/actionable findings
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_4e7cdf4d3606`, lease `cbx_23f3b594031f`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed trusted policy state no longer throws open or crashes diagnostics; unreadable or malformed policy inputs fail closed while valid no-op policy decisions stay compatible.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree rebased onto `origin/main` `94814f35168`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate after rebasing the trusted-policy slice onto current main at the time of the run.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: cherry-picked the pruned trusted-policy slice onto current main, ran focused host-hook and before-tool-call tests, formatter check, diff check, touched-file scrub, autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`. After `main` moved once more, rebased again and reran focused sanity plus final autoreview.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unreadable policy registries, unreadable policy entries, throwing policy evaluation, unreadable decisions, malformed concrete decisions, unreadable params, and guarded diagnostic policy state fail closed without breaking healthy policy chains.

Observed result after fix: a malformed trusted policy cannot silently allow a tool call or crash diagnostic emission before the assistant path can continue handling the failure.

What was not tested: no full live plugin install with a hostile trusted-policy plugin; behavior is covered by host-hook contract tests, before-tool-call tests, and the remote changed gate including extension typecheck lanes.
target.author: vincentkoc
